items.title: France confirms data breach at government agency that manages citizens’ IDs
items.description: The French government agency that issues and manages national IDs, passports, and other documents, announced that hackers stole the personal information of an unspecified number of citizens.

items.title: Apple fixes bug that cops used to extract deleted chat messages from iPhones
items.description: The iPhone and iPad bug allowed law enforcement using forensic tools to read messages that had long been deleted by the Signal app.

items.title: From the stage to the future: Where are Startup Battlefield’s alumni now?
items.description: We wanted to show you what happens after the confetti falls. We checked in with some of our recent alumni, many of whom have sat down with us on Build Mode: The Founder Survival Guide, TechCrunch's podcast for founders at every stage.

items.title: Google Cloud launches two new AI chips to compete with Nvidia
items.description: Google's newest TPUs are faster and cheaper than the previous versions. But the company is still embracing Nvidia in its cloud — for now.

items.title: Google turns Chrome into an AI co-worker for the workplace
items.description: Google brings Gemini-powered "auto browse" capabilities to Chrome for enterprise users, letting workers automate tasks like research, data entry, and more.

items.title: Threads is adding Live Chats to boost real-time engagement
items.description: Users can send messages, photos, videos, links, and emoji reactions in Live Chats.

items.title: Google makes an interesting choice with its new agent-building tool for enterprises
items.description: Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform takes an interesting approach: It is geared for IT and technical users.

items.title: AI Overviews are coming to your Gmail at work
items.description: The AI Overviews will offer instant summaries pulled from across multiple emails.

items.title: Rivian R2 production has started despite tornado damage to factory
items.description: The company's all-important mass-market EV is just about ready to start heading to customers.

items.title: Cosmetics giant Rituals confirms data breach of customer membership records
items.description: The cosmetics retailer, which counts 41 million customers in its membership data, declined to provide an accurate total number of customers affected.

items.title: Cathie Wood’s ARK makes its first lead investment in startup Lucra — and it isn’t AI
items.description: Lucra offers corporate loyalty programs reimagined as interactive esports. But winning ARK as a lead wasn't easy.

items.title: OpenAI teams up with Infosys to bring AI tools to more businesses
items.description: Infosys said the integration will be used to help its clients modernize software development, automate workflows, and deploy AI systems, initially focusing software engineering, legacy modernization, and DevOps.

items.title: UK government says 100 countries have spyware that can hack people’s phones
items.description: The U.K.'s cybersecurity chief warned that U.K. businesses and critical infrastructure are underestimating the threat from spyware attacks and other cyberthreats, with more governments having access to the powerful surveillance technology than ever.

items.title: Esther and Anne Wojcicki back new  healthcare accelerator, fund
items.description: Mary Minno launches early-stage startup accelerator program, Treehub, and an early-stage firm, AI Health Fund, aimed at backing startups working at the intersection of healthcare and AI.

items.title: X makes it more expensive to post links through its API
items.description: Posting links on X through its API is now 1,900% more costly.

items.title: Amazon Music partners with Bandsintown for concert listings
items.description: Users will be able to see live events of an artist on their Amazon Music profile.

items.title: AI is spitting out more potential drugs than ever. This startup wants to figure out which ones matter.
items.description: 10x Science has raised a $4.8 million seed round to help pharmaceutical researchers understand complex molecules.

items.title: Snap Map’s new ‘Place Loyalty’ badges will show the spots you visit most often
items.description: If you’re in the top 25% of users who visit a certain place, you’ll see your ranking on the Snap Map and can choose to share it with others.
